posts.title: Slurm Cheatsheet
posts.text: Cheatsheet中文版 English Version Official Documentation (not that useful) Most used commandSubmit a task1sbatch job_script.sh Check all job status1squeue -u username Cancel a job1scancel job_id Useful Comment to Request Resources in Job Script12345678910#SBATCH --account=[account]#SBATCH --ntasks=[number_of_tasks]#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=[number_of_cpus_per_task]#SBATCH --nodes=[number_of_nodes]#SBATCH --gres=gpu:[gpu_model (optional)]:[number_of_gpus]#SBATCH --partition [name_of_the_partition]#SBATCH --job-name=[job_name]#SBATCH --time=[maximum_running_time]#SBATCH --mem=[maximum_usable_memory]#SBATCH --output=[output_file] DON’T set the output as slurm.output, the job will stuck somehow.

posts.title: Hexo Configuration
posts.text: _config.yml ConfigurationUse ssh instead of https link when setting up deploy in _config.yml 1234deploy: type: git repo: git@github.com:username/username.github.io.git branch: main Troubleshoot for Permission to user1/repo.git denied to user2.MacOpen Keychain Access.app and delete corresponding saved password related to user2.

posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment

pages.title: YYX's Logger
pages.text: Yuxuan YeI am a PhD student at the Intelligent Information System Lab, University of Bristol. My research interests are mainly about Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). Before I started my PhD career, I did a master by research degree in the University of Bristol to assess myself whether enjoy the research life. Fortunately, I find doing research is not dull or miserable for me. I really enjoy the process of thinking, practicing, and attempting. In addition, I found my supervisor kind and supportive, therefore I decided to continue my research career as a PhD student in the same university.Before I came to the UK, I finished all my prior study in China. I received a Bachelor degree in Information and Computing Science at Beihang University, which is a top university in China. During the 4 years studying mathematics, I gradually realised that it was really not my cup of tea.

pages.title: Curriculum Vitae
pages.text: Education PhDin Computer Science University of Bristol 2022.03 - 2026.03 MScRin Computer Science University of Bristol 2020.11 - 2022.03 BScin Information and Computing Science Beihang University 2016.09 - 2020.06 I recently passed my PhD viva at the University of Bristol, where I had the privilege of being supervised by two super nice supervisors, Dr. Edwin Simpson and Prof. Raul Santos-Rodriguez. Prior to my PhD, I completed my Master of Science by Research (MScR), also under Dr. Edwin Simpson’s excellent supervision. My academic journey began at Beihang University, where I spent four wonderful years earning my undergraduate degree. Research InterestsMy research interests focus on factuality in abstractive summarisation, which directly decides the trustworthiness and applicability of generated summaries. In the LLM era, fluency and coherence is less concerned as before, but factuality remains an unsolved problem. Abstractive summarisation relies on explicitly provided source documents for grounding, therefore it eliminates the complexities of external document retrieval. This isolates the problem, allowing me to focus entirely on factuality research. My work spans two deeply intertwined directions: Factuality Alignment: Constructing factuality-focused signals and developing effective training pipelines to align summarisation models towards factuality. Factuality Evaluation: Developing robust and efficient metrics to accurately measure factual consistency. In both areas, I focus heavily on compute-efficient methodologies. My preferred approaches include preference-based training algorithms that efficiently adapts both large and small language models for use as both factual summarisers and factuality evaluation metrics. ServicesI served as a reviewer for many renowned conferences and journals, including EMNLP, AACL, EACL, ACL, JAIR. Publications Ye, Yuxuan, Raul Santos-Rodriguez, and Edwin Simpson. “Teaching Language Models to Check Grounded Claim Factuality with Human Test-Taking Strategies.”, Main Conference of ACL 2026. Ye, Yuxuan, Raul Santos-Rodriguez, and Edwin Simpson. “Optimising Factual Consistency in Summarisation via Preference Learning from Multiple Imperfect Metrics.” Findings of EMNLP 2025. Ye, Yuxuan, and Edwin Simpson. “Towards Abstractive Timeline Summarisation using Preference-based Reinforcement Learning.”, ECAI 2023. Ye, Yuxuan, Edwin Simpson, and Raul Santos Rodriguez. “Using similarity to evaluate factual consistency in summaries.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2409.15090 (2024). Guo, Zhijin, Chenhao Xue, Zhaozhen Xu, Hongbo Bo, Yuxuan Ye, Janet B. Pierrehumbert, and Martha Lewis. “Quantifying Compositionality of Classic and State-of-the-Art Embeddings.” Findings of EMNLP 2025. Ye, Yuxuan, and Edwin Simpson. “A proposal: Interactively learning to summarise timelines by reinforcement learning.” The First Workshop on Interactive Learning for Natural Language Processing, 2021. Fun FactI am a Badminton World Federation-registered badminton player, highest world ranking 1576 in XD (not very high).
